mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month. This relationship holds regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor demand that compresses consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE breaches the 4.5% threshold in walk-forward testing (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical instances where both INDPRO and HOUST decline concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary regime, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of declining real consumer credit on equity valuations, producing strong negative forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline when FEDFUNDS and RSAFS move inversely during rate-hiking periods, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window, or the sign of the relationship flips consistently.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 3.5% annualized, it signals an overheating economy that triggers inflationary pressures and subsequent monetary tightening, leading to negative equity returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when GDPC1 growth surpasses the 3.5% threshold, indicating the overheating signal does not translate to market declines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with accelerating inflation indicate excessive leverage and purchasing power erosion, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in walk-forward tests, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, strong payroll growth acts as a powerful mean-reversion signal that boosts risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not turn positive when PAYEMS growth accelerates while UNRATE remains above 5.0%, or the predictive power vanishes in a 3-year rolling evaluation.
